title: Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman: The $852 Billion Fight Over a Robot Brain
summary:
Imagine you and a friend start a lemonade stand. You agree all the money will go to help animals. Then your friend turns it into a huge business and keeps the profits. That's kind of what Elon Musk says happened with OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT. He's in a big courtroom battle with CEO Sam Altman.

Musk says back in 2015, he gave about $38 million to start OpenAI as a nonprofit (a charity that uses money for a good cause, not to make owners rich). He thought they promised to always be a nonprofit, working to help all of humanity. But by 2022, he worried Altman was trying to "steal the charity." Now OpenAI is worth $852 billion!

Altman's lawyers say there was never a promise to stay a nonprofit forever. They say Musk is just trying to slow down OpenAI so his own AI company, xAI, can catch up.

In court, things got a little weird. When a lawyer asked Musk tricky questions, he said, "Your questions are not simple. They are designed to trick me essentially." The judge even had to step in, and at one point Musk compared a question to asking "have you stopped beating your wife?" That made people laugh, but the judge told him to stop.

This trial could change who controls super-smart AI. If Musk wins, Altman might have to leave OpenAI's board, and the company's plans to sell shares to the public could be ruined. The trial is expected to last about four weeks.
why_it_matters: This trial could decide who controls super‑smart computer brains that might one day be in tools you use at school, like ChatGPT.

keywords.explanation: An organization that uses its money to do good things instead of making owners rich.
A room where a judge listens to both sides and decides legal arguments.
Small pieces of a company that people can buy, making them part owners.
A group of people who make big decisions for a company.
Computer programs that can learn and do tasks that normally need human smarts.
A formal process in court where both sides present their case and a judge or jury decides.

questions.question: What does Elon Musk say OpenAI was created to be?
How much money does Elon Musk say he gave to start OpenAI?
What did Musk say Altman was trying to do in 2022?
What does Musk's own AI company, xAI, want to do?
What did the judge tell Musk to stop doing during the trial?
options: Comparing a question to a wife‑beating joke
questions.correct_answer: Comparing a question to a wife‑beating joke

questions.question: How long is the trial expected to last?

background_read: OpenAI (the company behind the chatty robot ChatGPT) was started in 2015 with big dreams of making AI that helps all people.
Elon Musk, the billionaire who runs Tesla and SpaceX, helped pay for it but now says it broke its promise.
Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, leads the company today and wants it to grow into a huge business.
Article_Structure: WHO: Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.
WHAT: A courtroom fight over whether OpenAI must stay a nonprofit or can become a for‑profit company.
WHERE: In a courtroom in the United States.
WHY: Musk says OpenAI promised forever to be a charity helping all humanity, while Altman says no such promise was made.

perspectives.description: They believe AI should be shared freely and that turning OpenAI into a money‑making business could put profit ahead of safety.
They think Musk only wants to slow OpenAI down so his own AI company can win, and that businesses need money to keep improving.
This is a power struggle between two tech leaders, and the court’s decision will set rules for how future AI companies can grow.
